human_review.claim_limit: One reviewer is exploratory. A publishable comparative claim requires at least two independent reviewers, agreement reporting, and adjudication of disagreements.

comparison_to_all_scope.interpretation: Positive values favor Opus. The numeric-scope estimate is the fair trained-domain human comparison; the all-scope estimate remains the original, broader exploratory result.

good_at_3_proxy.note: This set-level rubric does not score every emitted distractor against all registered GDR gates. Issue flags are candidate-level and cannot be converted into pair-level all-gates pass/fail judgments.

human_gdr_proxy.note: This set-level rubric does not score every emitted distractor against all registered GDR gates. Issue flags are candidate-level and cannot be converted into pair-level all-gates pass/fail judgments.

inter_rater_agreement.note: At least two independent reviewers are required.

item_results.note: A has much less plausible answers.
The question is asking for the digit, not the value.
B has a bit of a duplicate with 2 answer that are almost the same.

item_results.topic: Rounding to the Nearest Whole (10, 100, etc)

item_results.note: A supplied nothing. B was bad. theyre both terrible.
A is a bit more plausible.

numeric_scope_interpretation.note: One reviewer and a post-review gold-defined subset; uncertainty is exploratory even when the paired interval excludes zero.

overall.averaging_method: Within each reviewed item, average the three paired rating differences; then average equally across items.

preference_contrast.scale: -1 = all prefer second; 0 = balanced/tied; +1 = all prefer first

response_consistency_audit.note: This mechanical audit flags response patterns for manual inspection; it does not prove reviewer intent or tampering.

overall_rating.averaging_method: Arithmetic mean across all three dimension ratings and all reviewed items; every item-dimension is equally weighted.
Arithmetic mean across all three dimension ratings and all reviewed items; every item-dimension is equally weighted.

protocol.numeric_equivalence: Exact Fraction equality after deterministic display parsing; percent/currency/measurement suffixes retain displayed magnitude.

roles.v8_best_of_n: four candidates plus deterministic verifier selection; a system result, not model-only

verdict.claim_boundary: Written, categorical, operation-choice, image-answer, compound, and unparseable keys are declared exclusions. Results are not overall MCQ superiority outside this numeric scope.
verdict.deterministic_hard_gates: v8 best-of-N is the numeric deterministic hard-gate winner: higher on schema, distinct answers, misconception-label distinctness, and computation, but lower on key safety. Model-only v8 has a demonstrated computation advantage and lower key safety; its answer-diversity difference is uncertain.
verdict.fair_scope: Numeric-only is the intended trained-domain comparison because eligibility is fixed from trusted gold before examining outputs.
verdict.human_evidence: The numeric blind subset remains exploratory: one reviewer, post-review gold-defined filtering, and an all-B response anomaly.
verdict.human_ratings: NO DEMONSTRATED HUMAN-RATING WINNER. Opus leads the overall point estimate by 0.33/5, but the paired 95% interval crosses zero.
verdict.registered_win_rule: NOT DEMONSTRATED because GDR/Good@3 remain unavailable.
